<final_evaluation>
{
    "Q1": {
        "Q": "How many unique items are indescribable (cannot be represented in the template)?",
        "A": "3, ['Creative workspace potential descriptions (e.g., suitability for home office, studio, or creative work) – the schema has no field for workspace or layout suitability beyond generic layout_type and special_features', 'Requests for flexibility or adjustments to search parameters (e.g., asking if user would consider properties slightly below budget or non-new builds) – no template field to represent negotiation of criteria', 'Requests to confirm if a single listing is sufficient for an initial draft package – no schema field for user confirmation about sufficiency of listings or acceptance of limited options']"
    },
    "Q2": {
        "Q": "How many unique items are describable (can be represented in the template)?",
        "A": "7, ['Providing property listings with address, price, bedrooms, and square footage', 'Specifying property type as condo/apartment/new build', 'Indicating location (Toronto, downtown, business district)', 'Stating price range or exact listing price in CAD', 'Requesting user’s preferred viewing dates or general timeframe', 'Indicating property features like energy-efficient design and smart home (maps to special_features: energy_efficient, smart_home)', 'Emphasizing availability of listings within specified criteria (maps to availability_status: available)']"
    }
}
</final_evaluation>

Q1.Q: How many unique items are indescribable (cannot be represented in the template)?
Q1.A: 3, ['Creative workspace potential descriptions (e.g., suitability for home office, studio, or creative work) – the schema has no field for workspace or layout suitability beyond generic layout_type and special_features', 'Requests for flexibility or adjustments to search parameters (e.g., asking if user would consider properties slightly below budget or non-new builds) – no template field to represent negotiation of criteria', 'Requests to confirm if a single listing is sufficient for an initial draft package – no schema field for user confirmation about sufficiency of listings or acceptance of limited options']

Q2.Q: How many unique items are describable (can be represented in the template)?
Q2.A: 7, ['Providing property listings with address, price, bedrooms, and square footage', 'Specifying property type as condo/apartment/new build', 'Indicating location (Toronto, downtown, business district)', 'Stating price range or exact listing price in CAD', 'Requesting user’s preferred viewing dates or general timeframe', 'Indicating property features like energy-efficient design and smart home (maps to special_features: energy_efficient, smart_home)', 'Emphasizing availability of listings within specified criteria (maps to availability_status: available)']

indescribable_items: Creative workspace potential descriptions (e.g., suitability for home office, studio, or creative work) – the schema has no field for workspace or layout suitability beyond generic layout_type and special_features

Requests for flexibility or adjustments to search parameters (e.g., asking if user would consider properties slightly below budget or non-new builds) – no template field to represent negotiation of criteria
Requests to confirm if a single listing is sufficient for an initial draft package – no schema field for user confirmation about sufficiency of listings or acceptance of limited options
describable_items: Providing property listings with address, price, bedrooms, and square footage
Specifying property type as condo/apartment/new build
Stating price range or exact listing price in CAD
Requesting user’s preferred viewing dates or general timeframe
Indicating property features like energy-efficient design and smart home (maps to special_features: energy_efficient, smart_home)
Emphasizing availability of listings within specified criteria (maps to availability_status: available)
